name: Extend QueryKey with language discriminator

scope_summary.primary_change: Added pub language: Language field to QueryKey struct in crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/salsa.rs. Hash/Eq derive picks it up automatically — keys now compare by (query_name, args_hash, language) tuple.
scope_summary.stop_threshold_check: PASS — exactly 2 source files modified (salsa.rs + daemon.rs), as mandated by dispatch-contract.json::stop_threshold.

tdd_protocol.red_failure_mode: compile error E0061: this function takes 2 arguments but 3 arguments were supplied — confirms the load-bearing signature change.
tdd_protocol.red_to_green_delta: QueryKey gained language field; QueryKey::new gained language parameter; all callers thread the resolved language. Pre-fix the test file would not compile (3rd arg unknown). Post-fix it compiles and both isolation assertions pass.

regression_test.name: test_daemon_caches_python_and_typescript_results_independently
regression_test.result: PASS — verifies end-to-end disjoint-slot caching across Python and TypeScript queries on the same project root.

validation.l2_daemon_cache_bench_test_release: PASS (12 tests, including bench_concurrent_access_latency under release-mode contention; debug-mode flake on perf threshold pre-existing and unrelated)

augmentation_2_critical_finding.title: M3a's QueryKey change DOES alter on-disk cache file binary representation
augmentation_2_critical_finding.summary: QueryKey is serialized into CacheFileData via serde_json (salsa.rs:486). Adding the `language` field makes pre-v0.3.1 cache files un-deserializable: load_from_file() will return a serde_json error on the missing `language` field of every entry's QueryKey.
augmentation_2_critical_finding.impact_without_m3b: Daemon panics or errors on first run after v0.3.0 -> v0.3.1 upgrade. Users who upgrade and have a populated cache file at $XDG_CACHE_HOME/tldr-cli/* hit DaemonError::InvalidMessage or a serde error before reaching cold-cache fallback.
augmentation_2_critical_finding.in_flight_compatibility: BROKEN until M3b lands. M3b adds schema_version: u32 to the cache header; on load, mismatch triggers graceful discard (return Self::with_defaults()) instead of bubbling up the deserialize error.
augmentation_2_critical_finding.co_ship_mandate_satisfied: Per dispatch-contract.json L11-12 + L198 (must_ship_in_same_release_commit=true) + L339, M3a is forbidden from shipping in v0.3.1 release commit without M3b. This commit is internal-only on `main`; release tagging waits for cluster-M3b, cluster-M2 (already landed eef5b31), and release-prep.
augmentation_2_critical_finding.recommendation_to_orchestrator: Launch v031-cluster-M3b worker IMMEDIATELY. M3b touches only salsa.rs (cache header path) and is bounded at <=1 source file + ~30 LOC. No additional surface change.

salsa.rs: Added `pub language: Language` to QueryKey struct (+ docstring documenting on-disk format coordination with M3b).
Updated `QueryKey::new` to take a third `language: Language` parameter.

Updated 55 internal unit/proptest QueryKey::new call sites (mechanical: appended `, Language::Python` to the existing 2-arg form via balanced-paren script). All preserved tests still validate the original (query_name, args_hash) hashing/eviction/persistence semantics — language is held constant per case so nothing in the existing assertion set changes meaning.
daemon.rs: Updated 16 QueryKey construction sites to thread language. Three categories:
(a) Variants WITH language field (Calls/Impact/Dead/Arch/Importers/ChangeImpact/Context, plus Warm/structure): use the resolved `lang` directly.

(b) Variants that auto-detect language from a file path (Extract/Cfg/Dfg/Slice/Imports/Structure): compute language via detect_or_parse_language BEFORE constructing QueryKey, then thread the detected language into the key.

(c) Truly language-agnostic variants (Search, Tree): tag with resolve_language(None) — the default fallback. Cache hits across languages are semantically valid for these because the result does not depend on language.

For Cfg/Dfg/Slice/Imports/Structure: reordered detect_or_parse_language() to BEFORE QueryKey::new so the detected language is available for the key. This fixes a latent design issue where the cache key was built before the language was known.
daemon_test.rs: Added new module `cluster_m3a_cache_isolation` with regression test `test_daemon_caches_python_and_typescript_results_independently` per dispatch-contract.json L213. Test exercises a mixed Python/TypeScript project, issues Calls{Python} then Calls{TypeScript} on the same root, and asserts (i) TS edges count > 0, (ii) at least one TS edge references a `.ts` file, (iii) the Python entry survives the TS query intact (re-querying Python returns the same value).
salsa_lang_isolation_test.rs: New test file. test_query_cache_isolates_by_language asserts py_key != ts_key for the same (query_name, args_hash), Python insert is observed only under Python lookup, TypeScript lookup misses. test_query_cache_two_languages_hold_distinct_values_for_same_args proves disjoint slots — both languages hold their own value simultaneously.
l2_daemon_cache_bench_test.rs: Mechanical: appended `, Language::Python` to 35 existing QueryKey::new call sites. Added `use tldr_core::Language;`. Bench semantics unchanged (single-language workload preserved).

notes: Hard cap of <= 2 source files respected: only salsa.rs + daemon.rs in src/.
Cargo.lock untouched (no version bumps, no dependency adds).
Zero references to sibling milestone files (types.rs, callgraph/*, rust_lang.rs, imports.rs, module_index.rs, state.rs).
M3b co-ship mandate flagged in `augmentation_2_critical_finding` for orchestrator action.
